reviews.review_description:
Nice, short little game, enjoyed the humor, but the "puzzles" are always the same.
Major flaw: The core mechanics of the game is to repeat, repeat, repeat a short period of time and everytime you decide to abort the run and retry, you have to click a button, then loading screen, then menu where you must re-turn to a certain direction, then press start, then you hit the intro sequence again, then you turn your hand vor the menu where you find the skip button which causes another second for fading to the real start of the situation - ah no, most of the time you have to wait another second or two until you can actually start manipulating objects.
I can't understand how a game that's core mechanic gets so annoying after the third repetition could have made it past play testers. Could have been at least on more star if you could retry with one button press and not more than two seconds loading time. Sad.
Fantastic. Well worth the price.

reviews.review_title: Not what you think it is
reviews.review_description: So please read this review if you want to know what the game really is, I honestly thought that this was going to be like a crew member on a ship doing maintenance. But it's really just protecting the captain from moving objects. It's a fun game don't get me wrong, but it's not actually what it looks like.
I don't see any way to enable smooth turning instead of snap turning. the game is fun,  but snap turning totally breaks immersion.
Playful and AAA. AI robots out of control and you have a chance to intercept the problem before it's too late.

I thought this game was cute. The little BOB robots remind me of the minions and the capitan is funny. I enjoyed the side quest challenges and I had to replay some of the episodes multiple times before figuring out the tricks and clever thinking that goes into trying to save 3 in 1. Good clean humor.

This is a really nice game, I love the captain!
It's true, it's not that long, but the main quests are there just to make you comfortable with the game, the difficult part are the side quests!there are a lot of funny moments, the grafics are very good and you see this is a game mede with love. The music is also good. 
I would like to see more games like this.

The game goes by quick, and there isn't much to do within the levels. You are confined to a small space and there is always a ticking clock, so you never really get to explore or experiment. Like the majority of VR games, it feels like a taste of something that could be much more, but will probably never be. And I'm not sure why there are so many VR games about robots. This game seems to feature a lot of played out VR tropes...
If you are new to VR, you will probably enjoy this and want to show it off to other VR newbies. If you have been playing VR for awhile now, it feels rather old, despite the neat premise.

Your main mission is to save the captain from each disaster.  Then as a side mission you need to save two helper bots.  Plus each level has bonus objectives which are only revealed if you’ve saved the captain once. Each level is very immersive and the mini bonus objectives add extra fun as some are obvious, and some need a real hard look around to work out what you need to do. I’m only 6 levels in and loving it.
It’s a good game. It is pleasant to feel like flash. But after a few level the game begins to become impossible.

reviews.review_title: I was very unsatisfied and want a refund
reviews.review_description:
I got this game thinking it was a fun puzzle game and saved up money to get $15.00 dollars! So I bought it and started playing it was great and all but then it just ended, apparently there are only 8 levels!! I got furious and depressed when I thought “Wow... you saved up $15.00 and spent hard work just for this game...” but then I remembered I can refund it. So I hit refund and it said the refund was requested. So about 2 days later and I went to look if I got a refund but it still said nothing so then I looked to see if I still had the game and I didn’t. And then went to go look if I could buy the game and I could buy the game. So then I thought I got a refund but it just hasn’t showed up yet. So I got 5 more dollars and bought a $20.00 combo pack that had two games for the price of one thinking I had a refund. I loved it and was very happy. About a day later I got on my oculus and then I saw Time Stall as one of my games I can play. Then my heart dropped. I looked to see my purchase history.... BOOM! It said that my refund was declined!! I got on the ground and started crying and was furious at the same time somehow. I was thinking “ I spent a flipping $35.00!!!” And now PLEASE OCULUS, GET ME A REFUND. I need it so I can put this money in to my savings. I’m going to need it to pay for college. So please get me a refund and get back to me ASAP.

PS: I also have already wrote one review and you guys said you didn’t understand so I hope this is good enough.
My wife and I really have a fun time playing this. The premise is very unique. A real hidden gem IMHO.
Tedious iterative problem-solving. Repetitive droning  music.  Childish mundane humor. Just awful.
Very fun puzzle game
Absolutely trash

Do not believe the 1 star reviews this an amazing game and a MUST BUY. Like yeah if are speed running the game of course it gonna be an hour long but if you actually try to fully beat the game you can get a decent 20 hours of gameplay depending on how good you are. When I first saw this game I thought it was gonna be lame but I was sooooooooooo wrong this game is a masterpiece. Props to the devs of this game for making this.

Edit: So I might have over aggregated my original one but I'm hear to fix all of that so first of all the mechanics/physics are great but the gameplay can become boring over time. The game will not last you 20 hours apparently I was just terrible at the game lol. There are some good comedian moments very there and there that will have you chuckle. The only down side is the game gets repetitive over time so that's why I sadly will have to change it to 4 stars but if this is ever on sale I would recommend buying this hidden gem. Still hoping for new content at some point but that would be rare

reviews.review_title: Great Concept, Just A Bit Buggy
reviews.review_description: I love the concept of this game, it’s like a much calmer and lighthearted version of Superhot, the only problem is, you can’t move around freely, and if you go outside of a certain border, the screen just goes black like it would with most games. The only problem with that is that with some levels (like the very last one) you have to go outside of the border to do something like unscrew a battery cover or grab something. You also can’t recenter yourself whatsoever or even set a place where you need to stand. I had to go all the way into the corner of my room to start a game. I’ve tried everything from holding the oculus button to resetting the game and nothing works. One other problem with it is that it was a very short game, with maybe 8 or 9 levels total and each level being about 2 minutes long, so if you don’t pace yourself, you’ll end up completing it way too fast but not fast enough to qualify for a refund. There’s no infinite playability unless you want to keep doing the same 9 levels over and over again, so I’d say this game was worth about 1/3 of its actual price.
About 1 hour in, really good concept, no glitches, objects are kind of hard to throw precisely but that’s to be expected with VR. Would definitely recommend

Ok so the gameplay it’s unique slowing down time is pretty cool in games especially in vr the story was good a soler flare hit the ship and now the robots are malfunction and out to kill you the captain and the bobs it not a horror game it’s more of a puzzle comedy game but it is kind of short though a dlc or a second part would be good p.s. thx to a wolf in vr for letting me know this exists so I can play it

reviews.review_title: Love it but needs a serious overhaul
reviews.review_description: So far after playing the game for an hour and a half its fun but annoying when the game wants me to rotate every time i go into a level and there appears to be a bug in the premium backer lack where a crackle sounds comes from the left speaker. Im playing on a quest 2
